committeeGateForm.allocationMatchHint: Must match the allocation assigned to your account for this conference.

staffBypassForm.title: SMT: not signing in as a delegate
staffBypassForm.description: Secretariat only. Enter the staff secondary password from organisers — not the committee password delegates share.

allocationCodeGateForm.codeHelp: This code is unique to your seat. Only one account can complete this step per seat — if you share an account, use that same login.

smtRoomCodesClient.committeeCodesHelp: Same value is used for the second gate (committee code) and the chair room join flow—unique within this event. Delegates enter it after the conference code.
smtRoomCodesClient.noCommitteesYet: No committees for this event yet.
smtRoomCodesClient.inviteHelp: Sends a Supabase magic link so they can set a password. Their profile is set to chair as soon as the invite is created. Configure the SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY server env variable and allow your sign-in URL (e.g. …/login) under Authentication -> URL configuration -> Redirect URLs.
smtRoomCodesClient.inviteDisabled: Invites are disabled until SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is set on this deployment.
smtRoomCodesClient.grantChairRoleHelp: If they already signed up as a delegate, promote them here so they get the chair dashboard and committee tools (no email sent).

chairDelegateApprovalForm.title: Accept delegate by email + allocation
chairDelegateApprovalForm.helpAcceptDelegateBody: Use this to directly assign a user account (by email) to a specific allocation in this conference.
chairDelegateApprovalForm.intro: Chairs can directly approve a delegate account to a specific allocation.
chairDelegateApprovalForm.delegateEmailHelp: Enter the exact account email the delegate uses to sign in.
chairDelegateApprovalForm.allocationHelp: Choose which country/seat to assign. "Currently assigned" means another user is already linked there.

roomCodeChairForm.committeeCodeSetupHelp: Pick the committee, set a 6-character code, and save. This updates the code delegates/staff use to enter that committee room.
roomCodeChairForm.searchPlaceholder: Type to filter by name or committee…
roomCodeChairForm.newCommitteeCodeHelp: Must be exactly 6 letters/numbers. Share this only with the committee participants who should access this room.

chairMotionsPointsLog.intro: Record and star points for delegates. Entries are saved to each delegate account/profile and are visible only to that delegate plus committee chair/SMT.
chairMotionsPointsLog.formalMotionsPrefix: Formal procedural votes and the motion floor are in
chairMotionsPointsLog.textPlaceholder: e.g. Motion to open the speaker list
chairMotionsPointsLog.disciplineRules: Rule mapping: 3 warnings => strike. Strike 1: no voting rights. Strike 2: speaking rights suspended. Strike 3: removed from committee.
chairMotionsPointsLog.reasonPlaceholder: e.g. repeated disruption during formal debate
chairMotionsPointsLog.presetHint: Tap a preset to add a point for the selected delegate.

runningNotesView.viewOnly: View-only (you can only edit your own running notes).
runningNotesView.noteNameHelp: Shown in the note list. If empty, tags or note text is used instead.
runningNotesView.googleDocsHelp: Paste a shared Google Docs link to show a live preview of your notes. The link is optional; you can keep plain text notes instead.
runningNotesView.googleDocsSaveHint: New Google Doc opens in another tab. Save after pasting the link; the preview updates as you type.
runningNotesView.readerWarning: Reader warning: this note may contain inappropriate language.
runningNotesView.saveHelp: Saves your note name, tags, running note text, and Google Docs URL (if provided) for your own account.

sessionControlClient.rollPresentVotingTitle: Present and voting — must vote, cannot abstain
sessionControlClient.presetExtendOpeningSpeech: Motion to Extend Opening Speech Time
sessionControlClient.presetOpenGsl: Motion to Open the General Speakers' List
sessionControlClient.presetForAgainstTitle: Motion to Begin For and Against Speeches on the Draft Resolution
sessionControlClient.presetSilentPrayerMotionTitle: Motion for a Minute of Silent Prayer or Meditation
sessionControlClient.presetRollCallVote: Motion for a Roll Call Vote
sessionControlClient.presetClauseByClauseTitle: Motion to Vote Clause by Clause
sessionControlClient.motionControlHelp: Chair workflow: capture motions, open one vote at a time, record votes by allocation, then close the motion to finalize pass/fail.
sessionControlClient.chairOnlyVotingHelp: Chair-only: one vote open at a time. Delegates do not vote in the app. Use Record votes to call each placard and record Yes or No only. Procedural motions do not use abstain. Pass/fail uses the required majority of members present on roll.
sessionControlClient.euGuidedPhaseHint: Core blockers remain guided: when a motion does not match this phase, chairs can still continue using an explicit override confirmation.
sessionControlClient.procedurePresetHelp: Prefills common motion types and default titles/timing fields. You can still edit details before opening.
sessionControlClient.noClausesFound: No clauses found for selected resolution.
sessionControlClient.daisAnnouncementsHelp: Use Markdown for bold, lists, and links. Pin one line for the floor strip; schedule a future publish time and delegates will only see it once that moment passes. You can edit or delete any line below.
sessionControlClient.rollCallHelp: Present: may abstain. Present and voting: must vote yes/no. Absent: no ballot is recorded.
sessionControlClient.rollCallIntro: For each delegate, choose Present (may abstain), Present and voting (must vote), or Absent.
sessionControlClient.noRollRowsYet: No roll rows yet. Initialize to create one row per delegate placard from this committee's allocation matrix.
sessionControlClient.delegateRollStatusHint: Use the three buttons to set that delegate's roll status. Current status is highlighted.
sessionControlClient.majorityHelp: Simple for procedural motions; two-thirds for resolutions, amendments, and motions to approve an amendment (Amendments preset).
sessionControlClient.motionerHelp: Optional. Set the delegation that moved the motion so records and exports show who introduced it.
sessionControlClient.mustVoteHelp: Marks this as a mandatory vote item according to your committee rules.
sessionControlClient.deleteMotionConfirm: Delete this motion permanently? All recorded votes for it will be removed and the motion will disappear from the record.
sessionControlClient.ballotsHelp: Ballots = placards with a recorded Yes or No (abstain is tracked separately and not counted in this denominator). Clear removes a recorded vote. For procedural motions, outcome compares Yes to members present on the roll (see roll call section).
sessionControlClient.recordVotesHelp: Chairs register votes per allocation. Abstain appears only for amendment/resolution votes when roll status is not Present and voting. During a roll-call-vote motion, you can capture Yes/No with Rights.
sessionControlClient.delegatesCannotVoteHelp: Delegates cannot vote in the app. Chairs record votes for each allocation. Abstain appears only for resolution/amendment votes when that delegation is not marked Present and voting.
sessionControlClient.noDelegatesSeatedYet: No delegates are seated for this committee yet.

server.committeeCodeNoMatch: No committee matches that code for this event. Enter the conference code first, then the exact 6-character committee code (SMT -> Room codes or Supabase -> conferences).
server.committeeCodeMustBeSix: Committee code must be exactly 6 letters or digits (e.g. ECO741).
server.onlyStaffSetCode: Only chairs, SMT, and admins can set committee codes.
server.chairSeatOnly: You can only set the committee code for a session where you have a seat.

voting.helpRequiredMajorityBody: Choose the vote threshold used to decide pass/fail. `Simple` is more than half. `2/3` requires a supermajority.
voting.helpMustVoteBody: Marks this motion as a “must-vote” item. This is used to communicate the requirement to participants (depending on your committee’s setup).
voting.delegateBlurb: The dais records votes in session (e.g. roll call). Motion votes here follow Yes/No only (abstain is not used). You can follow the tally; delegates do not cast votes in the app.
voting.noSeatedDelegates: No seated delegates available for this motion.

profile.name: Name
profile.customPronounNote: Your profile had a custom pronoun set that is no longer accepted here. Pick an option above (or leave blank). Saving replaces the old value.
profile.errPronounsPreset: Choose a pronoun from the list.
profile.errStorageSetup: Profile picture storage is not set up. In the Supabase project, run migration 00100_profile_pictures_storage_bucket.sql, or create a public storage bucket named profile-pictures (see README).
profile.errPublicUrl: Could not resolve public URL for uploaded image.

rop.title: Rules of Procedure (RoP)
rop.content:
# Rules of Procedure

## Points and Motions
- **Point of Order**: Correct procedure
- **Point of Information**: Question to speaker
- **Point of Personal Privilege**: Personal comfort
- **Motion to Table**: Postpone debate
- **Motion to Adjourn**: End session

## Voting
- Simple majority for procedural matters
- 2/3 majority for substantive matters
- Roll-call vote if requested

examples.title: Examples
examples.content:
# Examples

## Resolution Format
```
The General Assembly,
...
1. Calls upon member states to...
2. Urges the international community to...
```

## Position Paper Structure
1. Background
2. Country Policy
3. Proposed Solutions

templates.title: Templates
templates.content:
# Templates

## Chair Report Template
- Committee overview
- Key discussion points
- Resolutions passed
- Recommendations

chairReport.title: Chair Report
chairReport.content:
# Chair Report

Document committee proceedings and outcomes here.

guides.googleDocsHelp: New Google Doc opens in another tab. Paste a shareable link so delegates can open the doc here (view/edit depends on Google sharing).
guides.markdownOptional: Markdown notes (optional if you use a Google Doc)

glossary.title: MUN Agenda Glossary
glossary.subtitle: Unique terminology matched to your committee code and current agenda topics.

delegation.definition: A country or observer team representing one state or entity in committee.

placard.definition: Country sign used to request the floor, vote, and signal procedural motions.

dais.definition: Chairing team that moderates debate, applies procedure, and manages speaking order.

quorum.definition: Minimum number of delegations required to conduct formal business and votes.

motion.definition: Formal proposal to change the flow of debate, such as caucus type or speaking time.

point_of_order.definition: Intervention when procedure is being applied incorrectly under committee rules.

moderated_caucus.definition: Structured debate segment with short timed speeches on a defined subtopic.

unmoderated_caucus.definition: Informal negotiation period where delegates move freely to draft and merge ideas.

draft_resolution.definition: Working text of proposed committee action before final voting and adoption.

amendment.definition: Change proposed to draft text, either friendly or voted upon as unfriendly.

operative_clause.definition: Action-oriented numbered clause describing what the committee proposes to do.

preambulatory_clause.definition: Context-setting clause citing background principles, concerns, and prior frameworks.

right_of_reply.definition: Opportunity to respond to a direct statement that affected delegation dignity or position.

veto_power.definition: UNSC permanent-member authority to block substantive draft resolutions.

chapter_vii.definition: UN Charter authority on threats to peace, sanctions, and use-of-force measures.

sanctions_regime.definition: Coordinated restrictions used to influence behavior without direct military action.

arms_control.definition: Agreements and verification systems that limit types, quantities, or deployment of weapons.

non_proliferation.definition: Policies to prevent spread of nuclear, chemical, biological, or advanced weapon capabilities.

confidence_building_measures.definition: Transparency and communication tools that reduce misunderstanding and conflict risk.

universal_periodic_review.definition: Peer-review process where each UN member state reports and receives rights recommendations.

special_rapporteur.definition: Independent expert appointed to monitor and report on thematic or country rights issues.

treaty_body.definition: Committee of experts supervising implementation of a specific human-rights treaty.

public_health_emergency.definition: Serious outbreak event requiring urgent coordinated national and international response.

surveillance.definition: Continuous collection and analysis of health data to detect and track disease trends.

health_systems.definition: Improving workforce, financing, logistics, and governance for reliable healthcare delivery.

transnational_crime.definition: Criminal activity coordinated across borders through networks and illicit markets.

illicit_trafficking.definition: Illegal movement of drugs, arms, persons, or goods across jurisdictions.

rule_of_law_capacity.definition: Institutional ability of justice systems to investigate, prosecute, and protect rights fairly.

sustainable_development.definition: Growth model balancing social progress, economic opportunity, and environmental protection.

financing_for_development.definition: Public and private funding mechanisms that support long-term development priorities.

policy_coordination.definition: Alignment of state and agency actions to avoid duplication and increase collective impact.

child_protection.definition: Measures preventing exploitation, abuse, and violence against children in all settings.

education_access.definition: Ensuring inclusive, equitable, and continuous learning opportunities for all children.

humanitarian_response.definition: Rapid relief actions addressing urgent needs in crises and conflict settings.

gender_mainstreaming.definition: Integrating gender analysis into policy design, implementation, and evaluation.

gender_based_violence.definition: Harm directed at someone based on gender identity, roles, or unequal power relations.

economic_empowerment.definition: Expanding access to decent work, finance, ownership, and decision-making power.

source_attribution.definition: Citing information origin clearly so claims can be verified and contextualized.

verified_reporting.definition: Journalistic standard requiring corroboration before publishing contested facts.

editorial_independence.definition: Separation of reporting decisions from political pressure or stakeholder influence.

climate_mitigation.definition: Actions that reduce greenhouse-gas emissions or increase long-term carbon removal.

climate_adaptation.definition: Policies and investments that reduce vulnerability to climate-related impacts.

cyber_norms.definition: Shared expectations for responsible state behavior in digital and cyber domains.

critical_infrastructure.definition: Essential systems such as power, water, health, and communications requiring resilience.

epidemiology.definition: Study of disease patterns, causes, and distribution in populations.

preparedness_response.definition: Planning, coordination, and surge actions to contain and manage emergencies.

non_refoulement.definition: Principle prohibiting return of people to places where they face serious harm.

durable_solutions.definition: Long-term outcomes for displaced people: return, local integration, or resettlement.

nuclear_safeguards.definition: Verification measures ensuring nuclear materials are not diverted for weapons use.

counter_terrorism.definition: Integrated legal, preventive, and security measures against terrorist activity.

due_process.definition: Fair legal procedures protecting rights during investigation, prosecution, and sanctioning.

resource_security.definition: Reliable access to food, water, and energy under economic and environmental stress.

resilience.definition: Capacity of communities and institutions to absorb shocks and recover effectively.

ideas.addIntro: Add a Google Doc to draft a resolution idea in place, or type a short note (or both).
ideas.googleDocsCreateHelp: New Google Doc opens in another tab; paste the link here after it is created.

stances.heatmapHelp: To what extent does your allocation support ___? (1-5 scale)
stances.extentHelpBody: 1 means your allocation has little support for the topic, and 5 means strong support. Pick a number that matches your stance in the committee.
stances.noteLanguageWarning: Reader warning: this note may contain inappropriate language.
stances.saveHelpBody: Saves the stance note for this allocation. It can be viewed by staff and (depending on committee settings) other participants.

speeches.googleDocsHelp: Opens Google in a new tab; paste the document URL here when ready.
speeches.plainTextOptional: Plain text draft (optional if you use a Google Doc)
speeches.noSpeechesYet: No speeches yet. Link a Google Doc or paste text.
speeches.noContentHint: Link a Google Doc with Edit, or add plain text in the form.

committeeRoom.clearSearchTitle: Clear search and show all delegations

committeeRoom.searchHint: Type a country, delegate name, school, pronoun, or "vacant" to highlight matches in the room. Press Enter to jump to the first match; Esc clears. Click Vacant to jump to empty seats.

requestToSpeak.title: Request to speak
requestToSpeak.needAllocation: You need an allocation to request to speak.
requestToSpeak.alreadyQueued: You already have an active speaking request in the queue.
requestToSpeak.requested: Requested to speak. You will appear in the queue.
requestToSpeak.helpBody: Sends your delegation to the chair queue. You can have one active waiting/current request at a time.
requestToSpeak.purposeHelpBody: This text is appended to your country name so chairs can see why you are requesting the floor.
requestToSpeak.rollPromptBodyPickStatus: セッションが開始されました。発言と投票を有効にするため、点呼ステータスを選択してください。この選択は変更できません。
requestToSpeak.rollPromptBodyWaitSessionStart: Waiting for the chair to start the committee session.
requestToSpeak.rollPromptBodyMarkedAbsent: You’re currently marked absent. Ask the chair to mark you Present to speak and vote.

motionVoting.help: The dais records each delegation's vote in session. Motion votes use Yes or No only (no abstain). Totals count only placards where Yes or No was recorded; absent delegations are skipped.

committeeControl.sessionNameHint: Saves automatically and appears in session history.

history.title: Session history
history.permissionsHint: Chairs can rename entries. SMT/Admin can rename and delete entries.

timerPage.title: Timer
timerPage.controlsHelp: Use General floor for overall debate timing, or Motion vote to tie visibility to a specific open vote item.
timerPage.setupHelp: Choose whether this timer is for general floor (always visible to delegates) or tied to the motion open for voting (delegates only see it while that vote is the active item). With per-speaker time on, the clock stays visible during moderated caucus regardless. Pause clock / Start clock freeze or resume the countdown. Set Speaker time (remaining) and Total time independently, then Save timer. Presets fill both to the same length; you can edit either field afterward. Pauses are logged with a short reason.
timerPage.purposeGeneralFloor: General debate / floor (not tied to a vote)
timerPage.purposeMotionVote: Open motion - timer only while that vote is active on the floor
timerPage.openMotionForVoting: Open motion (must be open for voting)
timerPage.noOpenMotionWarning: No motion is open for voting. Open a vote first, or switch purpose to general floor.
timerPage.saveToEnablePauseStart: save the timer to enable pause/start.
timerPage.pauseReasonPlaceholder: e.g. Point of order, tech issue, unmoderated caucus...
timerPage.perSpeakerHelp: Set Total time as the per-speaker cap and Speaker time as time remaining; Advance moves to the next speaker and resets remaining to the cap.
timerPage.speechNotesHelp: Notes are tied to the Current speaker timer text and, when the speaker list has someone marked Current, their delegation is stored for reference.
timerPage.noCurrentDelegation: No delegation is marked Current on the speaker list.
timerPage.remainingHelpPerSpeaker: Countdown for the current speaker (resets when you Advance).
timerPage.remainingHelpNormal: Countdown for the current speaker (can be less than total).
timerPage.totalHelpPerSpeaker: Per-speaker cap (denominator on the floor; Advance refills remaining to this).
timerPage.totalHelpNormal: Full length of this timer segment (shown after the slash on the floor).
timerPage.noPauseEvents: No pause events logged yet for this committee.
timerPage.openPauseLogHint: Open Pause log to review recorded pause reasons and timestamps.
timerPage.savedCapped: Timer saved. Speaker time was longer than total; capped to total.

agendaTopics.qClimateCooperation: How can member states strengthen global cooperation on climate action?
agendaTopics.qGlobalHealthcareAccess: How can the international community improve access to quality healthcare?
agendaTopics.qAiGovernance: How should states regulate AI and emerging technologies?
agendaTopics.qDisinformationImpact: What measures can reduce the global impact of disinformation?
agendaTopics.qSecurityHumanRights: How can countries balance security and human rights in counter-terrorism?
agendaTopics.qRefugeeProtection: How can the UN system support refugee protection and durable solutions?
agendaTopics.qFoodSecurity: What strategies can improve food security in climate-vulnerable regions?
agendaTopics.qNonProliferation: How should the international community prevent nuclear proliferation?

delegationNotes.title: Delegation notes
delegationNotes.concernHelpBody: Use this when your note includes a moderation-sensitive concern. The reader may see a warning, but your note will still send.
delegationNotes.sessionOnlyHint: Notes are available only during active committee sessions.
delegationNotes.unmoderatedHint: Notes are disabled during unmoderated caucus.
delegationNotes.sendHelpBody: Sends this note to the recipients selected above. It doesn’t prevent sending; it only affects how the reader is warned/flagged.
delegationNotes.noDelegations: No assigned delegations for this committee.
delegationNotes.delegateTargetsHint: Targets are limited to assigned allocations only.
delegationNotes.readerWarning: Reader warning: this note may contain inappropriate language.

errors.votingProcedure: Voting procedure is active: note composing is disabled.
errors.sessionInactive: Notes are disabled while the committee session is not active.
errors.unmoderated: Notes are disabled during unmoderated caucus.
errors.smtVerify: Verify with the staff secondary password to send notes to this committee.
errors.noRecipients: Select at least one recipient (delegation or chair).
errors.needAllocation: You need an allocation to send notes (or be a chair).

adminPage.title: Website administration
adminPage.intro: Create conference events and manage who can run the secretariat tools. Day-to-day committee oversight lives in the SMT dashboard.
adminPage.noSessionFloor: The session floor (timers, speakers, roll call) is for dais chairs only. Use SMT dashboard for live committees, or enter committee codes like delegates when you need a specific session.

adminDashboard.newEventBody: Creates a conference code (first gate) and the first committee session (second gate). Use next=/admin so you return here after setup.
adminDashboard.smtBody: Invite new SMT accounts by email, or change an existing user to delegate, chair, or SMT. Promoting someone to SMT lets them use the full secretariat dashboard during the event.
adminDashboard.inviteNeedsKey: Email invites need SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY on the server. You can still use Set role for users who already signed up.

adminStatusPortal.title: Status portal
adminStatusPortal.noSessionsYet: No committee sessions found for this event yet.
adminStatusPortal.intro: Delegates move from Pending to Confirmed after approval. Confirming provisions them as a delegate and triggers the Welcome email.

conferenceSetupPage.title: Set up a conference
conferenceSetupPage.intro: Secretariat or website admin: creates a conference code (first gate) and the first committee code (second gate). Add more committees in Supabase or via your workflow; dais chairs edit committee codes under Chair → Committee code.

conferenceSetupForm.eventNameHelp: Shown to organisers; delegates see it after they join.
conferenceSetupForm.eventCodeHelp: Everyone enters this first (whole conference). Spaces ignored; stored in capitals. Must be unique.
conferenceSetupForm.sessionNamePlaceholder: e.g. Topic or agenda title for this committee
conferenceSetupForm.sessionNameHelp: Main heading for this committee in the app header.
conferenceSetupForm.committeeHelp: Optional. Shown next to the session title.
conferenceSetupForm.committeeCodeHelp: Second gate: exactly six letters or digits, unique within this event—typically three letters from the chamber name plus three digits (e.g. ECOSOC → ECO741). Non-alphanumeric characters are stripped when you save.
conferenceSetupForm.passwordIntro: Delegates confirm allocation + this password after login. Leave blank to skip.
conferenceSetupForm.committeePasswordConfirmPlaceholder: Same as above if using a password

committeeGate.requiredConferenceAllocationPassword: Conference, allocation, and password are required.
committeeGate.contextMismatch: Your committee context does not match this sign-in. Re-enter your conference and committee codes if needed.
committeeGate.conferenceSelectionMismatch: Your conference selection does not match this committee. Re-enter your conference and committee codes.
committeeGate.conferenceSelectionMismatchWithBackHint: Your conference selection does not match this committee. Go back and enter the correct conference code, then committee code.
committeeGate.noAllocationForConference: You have no allocation for this conference. Ask a chair to assign you before continuing.
committeeGate.allocationMismatch: Allocation does not match your assignment. Enter your country or position exactly as assigned (spacing and accents may differ).
committeeGate.requiredConferenceAndStaffPassword: Conference and staff secondary password are required.
committeeGate.onlySmtOrAdminBypass: Only secretariat or website admins can use this sign-in.
committeeGate.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 6 characters.
committeeGate.onlyChairSmtAdminSetPassword: Only chairs, SMT, and website admins can set the committee password.
committeeGate.onlyChairSmtAdminRemovePassword: Only chairs, SMT, and website admins can remove the committee password.

smtAllocations.chooseEventFirst: Choose an event with the conference code (event gate) first.
smtAllocations.onlySmtManageMatrix: Only secretariat can manage the allocation matrix.
smtAllocations.committeeNotInActiveEvent: This committee is not part of the active event.
smtAllocations.seatLinkedToDelegate: This seat is linked to a delegate. Unlink them in Supabase or reassign before deleting.
smtAllocations.noDataRowsFound: No data rows found (need at least a country/position per line).
smtAllocations.importLimitedRows: Import limited to {max} rows. Split your file.

smtStaffAccess.chooseEventFirst: Choose an event with the conference code (event gate) first.
smtStaffAccess.onlySmtChangeCodes: Only secretariat can change committee codes here.
smtStaffAccess.committeeNotInActiveEvent: This committee is not part of your active event.
smtStaffAccess.invalidCommitteeCode: Committee / room code must be exactly 6 letters or digits (e.g. ECO741).
smtStaffAccess.codeSaved: Code saved. Delegates use it as the second gate after the conference code.
smtStaffAccess.onlySmtInviteChair: Only secretariat can send chair invites.
smtStaffAccess.missingServiceRoleKey: Server is missing SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY. Add it to the deployment env (never expose to the browser).
smtStaffAccess.missingPublicAppUrl: Set NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL to your public site URL (e.g. https://your-app.vercel.app) so invite links redirect correctly.
smtStaffAccess.emailAlreadyHasAccount: That email already has an account. Use "Grant chair role" below instead of sending another invite.
smtStaffAccess.inviteSentButRoleSetFailed: Invite sent, but could not set chair role on profile: {error}. Use "Grant chair role" after they accept.
smtStaffAccess.inviteSentSuccess: Invite email sent to {email}. They will be a chair after they set their password.
smtStaffAccess.onlySmtGrantChair: Only secretariat can grant chair roles.
smtStaffAccess.noAccountForEmail: No account with that email yet. Send a chair invite first.
smtStaffAccess.promotedToChairSuccess: Profile for {email} is now a dais chair.

allocationSignup.onlyDelegateOrChair: Only delegate/chair accounts can request allocation sign-up.
allocationSignup.allocationAlreadyAssigned: That allocation has already been assigned.
allocationSignup.requiredConferenceAllocationEmail: Conference, allocation, and delegate email are required.

exportMaterials.onlyDelegatesCanExport: Only delegates can export their materials.
exportMaterials.accountHasNoEmail: Your account has no email address set.
exportMaterials.emailSendConfigHint: Set SMTP_HOST/SMTP_PORT/SMTP_USER/SMTP_PASS and optionally MATERIALS_EXPORT_FROM on the server.
exportMaterials.exportEmailedTo: Export emailed to {email}. If you don't see it, check spam/junk.

allocationSignup.welcomeConfirmedFor: Your delegate account has been confirmed for: {country}.
allocationSignup.welcomeLine2: You can now proceed to committee sign-in and join the live session.
allocationSignup.chairReminderLine1: A delegate has requested an allocation and needs chair review.
allocationSignup.chairReminderLine2: Please approve or reject this request in the chair allocation matrix:

smtConferenceSettings.selectConferenceFirst: Select a conference with the event gate first, then return here.
smtConferenceSettings.committeeSessionsBody: Committee session titles (agenda topics) stay here for records; the SMT home grid shows the official committee name, acronym, chairs, and a short display code. Committee / room codes (second gate) must stay unique within this event.
smtConferenceSettings.noCommitteeSessions: No committee sessions for this event.
smtConferenceSettings.errorPublicUrl: Could not resolve public URL for uploaded image.
smtConferenceSettings.chairNamesPlaceholder: Comma-separated, e.g. Alex Kim, Jordan Lee
smtConferenceSettings.crisisSlidesHelpPrefix: For FWC / UNSC / HSC: embedded on delegates' and chairs'
smtConferenceSettings.crisisSlidesHelpSuffix: page. Leave blank if not used. Deck should allow viewing for your audience (link sharing).
smtConferenceSettings.competingMotionsTitle: Competing motions (RoP): consultation before moderated caucus
smtConferenceSettings.competingMotionsHelpPrefix: Uncheck if your handbook ranks a
smtConferenceSettings.euGuidedWorkflowHelp: Enables EU Parliament phase guidance and core procedural blockers. Ignored unless procedure profile is set to EU Parliament.
smtConferenceSettings.committeeCodeHelpPrefix: Six characters: chamber initials + three digits (deterministic suggestion:

allocationMatrixManager.confirmRemoveUnassignedSeat: Remove this unassigned seat from the matrix?
allocationMatrixManager.skippedDuplicates: Skipped {count} duplicate(s) already on the roster.
allocationMatrixManager.generatedRandomCodes: Generated {count} random code(s) for rows without one.
allocationMatrixManager.addCommitteesFirst: Add committees to this event first in
allocationMatrixManager.showingOneCommitteeAtATime: Showing one committee at a time. Switch committee using the bottom tabs.
allocationMatrixManager.linkedDelegatesCannotBeDeleted: Linked delegates cannot be deleted here.
allocationMatrixManager.noSeatsYet: No seats yet. Add rows or import a CSV.
allocationMatrixManager.importHelpMiddle: A header row with "country" is fine. UTF-8 files from Excel/Google Sheets export work.
allocationMatrixManager.appendHelp: skips names already on this committee.
allocationMatrixManager.replaceUnassignedHelp: removes every open seat (not linked to a delegate), then loads the file while keeping linked delegates safe.

awardsRubric.tierPromptPick: Pick low or high within {band} (points on the 1-8 scale).

bands.0: Proposes repetitive or standard solutions; rarely thinks outside the existing framework.
bands.1: Offers some original ideas but struggles to adapt them to changing committee dynamics.
bands.2: Frequently suggests innovative solutions and unique clauses for draft resolutions.
bands.3: Highly creative; develops "game-changing" compromises that bridge clashing blocs.

bands.0: Lacks professional decorum; occasionally dismissive of other delegates' viewpoints.
bands.1: Respectful but unremarkable; maintains a neutral presence without building rapport.
bands.2: Consistently professional; actively seeks to understand and incorporate opposing views.
bands.3: Exemplifies true statesmanship; commands respect while remaining humble and inclusive.

bands.0: Works in isolation or refuses to compromise on minor details; disrupts group work.
bands.1: Contributes to a bloc but does not take an active role in drafting or merging ideas.
bands.2: A strong team player; helps merge resolutions and ensures all bloc members have a voice.
bands.3: The "glue" of the committee brings disparate groups together and facilitates consensus.

bands.0: Passive; waits for others to initiate motions or start discussions during caucuses.
bands.1: Shows leadership in small groups but is hesitant to lead the house or present for the bloc.
bands.2: Takes clear initiative; leads unmoderated caucuses and manages the drafting process.
bands.3: Visionary leader; sets the tone for the room and inspires others through action and guidance.

bands.0: Frequently confused by the topic; relies on generalities rather than specific facts.
bands.1: Has a basic understanding of the agenda but misses technical or legal nuances.
bands.2: Demonstrates strong command of the topic; cites relevant stats and UN past actions.
bands.3: Expert-level mastery; uses deep research to navigate technical debates and debunk false info.

bands.0: Rarely speaks; frequently absent during caucusing or inactive during voting.
bands.1: Speaks occasionally in moderated caucuses; participates only when prompted.
bands.2: Consistently active in all sessions; frequently raises motions and contributes to the floor.
bands.3: Necessary and consistent presence; engages in every aspect of the debate from start to finish.

bands.0: Minimal data; lacks specific UN resolutions, treaty citations, or historical context.
bands.1: Basic data provided; mentions well-known treaties but lacks specific localised evidence.
bands.2: Strong research; includes relevant stats, past UN actions, and committee-specific history.
bands.3: Exceptional depth; identifies niche legal loopholes, specific funding gaps, or rare data points.

bands.0: Frequently contradicts the assigned country's real-world geopolitical interests or voting history.
bands.1: Generally follows policy but lacks clarity on sensitive or controversial national stances.
bands.2: Consistently accurate; clearly reflects the nation's strategic regional and global interests.
bands.3: Highly nuanced; addresses complex regional dynamics and clearly defines national "red lines."

bands.0: Fundamental misunderstanding of the topic's legal framework or the committee's mandate.
bands.1: Understands the general topic but misses technical or legal complexities within current policy.
bands.2: Solid grasp of complex policy issues (e.g., specific clauses in international law).
bands.3: Expert-level accuracy; integrates technical facts to build a sophisticated policy argument.

bands.0: Vague or non-actionable (e.g., "countries should talk more"). No implementation plan.
bands.1: Generic solutions; lack details on funding, specific UN agencies, or feasibility.
bands.2: Innovative and actionable; proposes specific mechanisms, task forces, or monitoring bodies.
bands.3: Sophisticated and holistic; solutions are original, feasible, and legally sound with clear timelines.

bands.0: Significant errors in UN citation style (e.g., Chicago/APA); unprofessional tone.
bands.1: Standard formatting, but contains several grammatical gaps or inconsistent citation styles.
bands.2: Professional UN academic formatting; clear, concise, and persuasive diplomatic language.
bands.3: Flawless UN academic style; compelling narrative and perfect citation of all sources.

bands.0: Study guide was late or incomplete; minimal communication with SMT/USGs.
bands.1: The study guide met basic requirements but lacked deep analysis or clear formatting.
bands.2: Comprehensive study guide; proactive in answering delegate emails and prep.
bands.3: Exceptional study guide; provided extra resources and early feedback on position papers.

bands.0: Dismissive of delegate anxieties; fails to notice when a delegate is struggling or excluded.
bands.1: Professional but clinical; provides support only when explicitly asked by a delegate.
bands.2: Actively checks in on quiet delegates; creates a safe space for beginners to participate.
bands.3: High emotional intelligence; anticipates delegate stress, manages heated conflicts with care, and ensures every voice feels valued.

bands.0: One chair dominates; lack of communication results in inconsistent rulings.
bands.1: Work is split but chairs operate in silos; minimal consultation on award decisions.
bands.2: Balanced partnership; clear communication and mutual support during sessions.
bands.3: Seamless synergy; chairs anticipate each other's needs and provide a unified, professional front.

bands.0: Frequent errors in Rules of Procedure (RoP); relies heavily on the Secretariat for help.
bands.1: Basic RoP knowledge; can run a standard moderated caucus but misses nuances of motions.
bands.2: Strong command of RoP; manages the floor efficiently and fairly with minimal errors.
bands.3: Absolute mastery; uses RoP to enhance debate flow and handles complex procedural situations with ease.

bands.0: Unable to identify if a delegate is speaking "out of character"; ignores policy accuracy.
bands.1: Generally aware of major power stances but misses shifts in smaller delegation policies.
bands.2: Actively monitors policy accuracy; guides delegates back to character when necessary.
bands.3: Deeply researched in all allocations; provides precise feedback on geopolitical nuances during debate.

bands.0: Rigid adherence to the schedule even when debate is stagnant; resistant to SMT feedback.
bands.1: Adapts only when prompted by the Secretariat; struggles with sudden changes in bloc dynamics.
bands.2: Responds well to committee shifts; adjusts speaking times or motions to benefit the debate flow.
bands.3: Highly adaptive; pivots seamlessly during crises or unexpected debate turns while maintaining order.

bands.0: Unprofessional attire or tone shows clear bias toward specific delegates or schools.
bands.1: Professional but lacks authority; occasional lapses in diplomatic decorum.
bands.2: Consistently professional; maintains impartiality and commands respect from the house.
bands.3: Exemplifies the Secretariat standard; flawless decorum, impartial, and serves as a role model.

bands.0: Appears bored or distracted; minimal feedback given to delegates during caucusing.
bands.1: Attentive but passive; does not actively encourage quieter delegates to participate.
bands.2: High energy; provides constructive feedback and keeps the committee motivated throughout.
bands.3: Inspiring presence; actively mentors delegates, fosters an inclusive environment, and drives high-level debate.

bands.0: Surface-level summary; lacks historical context or data.
bands.1: Good overview but misses complex legal or technical nuances.
bands.2: Strong analysis; includes specific past actions and regional data.
bands.3: Expert-level scholarship; identifies unique angles for debate.

bands.0: Vague "Questions to Consider"; unclear committee powers.
bands.1: Standard questions; general guidance on the scope of the debate.
bands.2: Clear, focused questions that guide delegates toward specific solutions.
bands.3: Precision-engineered prompts that prevent stagnant or off-topic debate.

bands.0: Overly dense or disorganized; difficult for beginners to navigate.
bands.1: Clean layout but lacks visual aids or a glossary of terms.
bands.2: Well-structured; includes helpful links, maps, and a clear glossary.
bands.3: Masterfully designed; balances high-level academia with intuitive navigation.

bands.0: Few or outdated links provided for delegate research.
bands.1: Standard UN links provided; covers the basics for most nations.
bands.2: Diverse and curated bibliography including NGOs, treaties, and data sets.
bands.3: Comprehensive toolkit; provides delegates with "hidden" data and niche sources.

bands.0: Provides only a generic definition of the committee's name.
bands.1: Lists the mandate but fails to explain the committee's specific powers or limitations.
bands.2: Clearly outlines the committee's history, scope of authority, and role within the UN system.
bands.3: Masterfully connects the committee's specific legal "teeth" to the current agenda items.

bands.0: Vague or overly broad; fails to define the core problem.
bands.1: Provides a general summary but lacks current data or recent developments.
bands.2: Comprehensive overview; defines technical terms and the current global status of the issue.
bands.3: A compelling narrative that identifies specific "blind spots" in international policy.

bands.0: Missing major historical milestones or treaties.
bands.1: Lists basic dates but fails to explain the significance of past actions.
bands.2: Accurate chronological flow; explains how past resolutions led to the current situation.
bands.3: Strategic timeline that highlights failed vs successful interventions to guide future debate.

bands.0: Only mentions P5 nations; ignores regional blocs or minor stakeholders.
bands.1: Groups countries broadly (e.g. "The West") without nuanced policy differences.
bands.2: Details the specific priorities of major interest groups and key regional players.
bands.3: Sophisticated analysis of clashing "red lines" and potential spoilers in the negotiation process.

bands.0: Generic or repetitive (e.g. "more funding," "awareness").
bands.1: Suggests logical steps but lacks details on implementation or oversight.
bands.2: Proposes innovative, multi-dimensional frameworks aligned with the committee's mandate.
bands.3: High-level "Purposeful Policy" solutions that address root causes and technical feasibility.

bands.0: Frequent lulls in debate; circular arguments with no progress.
bands.1: Steady debate but struggles to move beyond "Topic A" generalities.
bands.2: High-level discourse; delegates move quickly into substantive solutions.
bands.3: Dynamic and evolving debate; the house stays engaged and intellectually sharp.

bands.0: Highly toxic or cliquey; minor blocs refuse to speak to one another.
bands.1: Standard diplomatic behavior; basic cooperation during caucusing.
bands.2: Inclusive atmosphere; larger blocs actively seek input from minor nations.
bands.3: Exemplary diplomacy; high "purposeful policy" alignment and consensus-building.

bands.0: Shallow resolutions; "vague encouragement" rather than action.
bands.1: Standard clauses; addresses the symptoms but not the root causes.
bands.2: Technically sound resolutions with clear funding and oversight mechanisms.
bands.3: Sophisticated, innovative, and actionable documents that reflect real UN standards.

bands.0: Chairs struggle to manage the room; low respect for the dais.
bands.1: Functional relationship; chairs follow the schedule but don't lead.
bands.2: Positive energy; chairs mentor delegates while maintaining firm order.
bands.3: Seamless synergy; the dais inspires a professional and high-stakes environment.
